observations.recommendation:
The LSB to –

Strictly follow the prescribed format in the presentation of the Income and Expenditure Estimates and observe the indicative timeline in the preparation, review and approval of the SEF Budget per DepEd-DILG-DBM JC No. 1, series of 2017;

Stop/discontinue allocating funds relating to prior year expenditures in the current year SEF annual budget; and

The City Accountant to strictly adhere to the accrual basis of accounting in accordance with IPSAS 1, and prepare the necessary adjusting entries to correct the recording of previous year’s expenses.
observations.managementAction:
Compliance with DepEd-DILG-DBM JC No. 1, Series 2017 was adhered to in the preparation of the CY 2022 SEF Annual Budget with due regard in observing the prescribed format and timeline.   




Allocation for (prior year) continuing appropriation is properly presented in the current year SEF annual budget.




Appropriate corrective measures have been adopted, henceforth, strict adherence to IPSAS 1 on accrual basis of accounting is strictly observed.
observations.reason: The same observation was noted in CY 2021.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the LCE direct the LSB to:
Strictly adhere to the indicative timeline in the preparation, review and approval of the proposed SEF Budget and observe the prescribed format in the presentation of the Income and Expenditure Estimates;
Include/present in the SEF/LSB annual budget the itemized and/or make reference on the breakdown of projects charged against the continuing appropriations; and
Henceforth, comply with the pertinent provisions of the DepEd-DILG-DBM JC No. 1, s. 2017.

We recommended that the LCE direct:
The CBO to closely monitor and reflect/record in the SAAOB – Continuing Appropriations the obligations pertaining to capital outlay chargeable against continuing appropriations;
The LSB to pass supplemental budget or LSB resolution in cases of realignment, reversion and reprogramming of appropriations; and
Henceforth, strictly comply with the prescribed laws, rules and regulations on the allocation and utilization of the SEF and observe proper accounting thereof.

We recommended that the LCE:
Require the City Engineer to: (i) request status of their letters sent to DPWH and make representation with the DPWH District Engineer to facilitate the repair of the lopsided/collapsed sheet pile. Afterwards, the City may proceed with the implementation of the project thereat; and (ii) strictly comply with the requirements of Section 17.6 of RA No. 9184, in relation with Annex A, Item 1 if the 2016 IRR of RA No. 9184 on the conduct of feasibility or preliminary engineering study to establish the technical viability of the project before bidding and awarding of the Contract for a particular project, moving forward; and
Inform the DBM about the problem encountered in the implementation of the project and actions taken to remedy the situation to avoid reversion of the fund to the National Treasury as provided in LBC Circular 116A.

We recommended that the LCE:
Require the LDRRMC to include in the LDRRMFIP the allocation and utilization of the specific PPAs charged against the unexpended balance of the LDRRMF and submit the revised LDRRMFIP as approved by the LCE and the Local Sanggunian, moving forward;
Direct the City Accountant and the City Treasurer to perform reconciliation of the recorded balances of the Trust Liability-DRRMF and Cash in Bank-Calamity Fund in the TF books, and make appropriate adjustment/s as necessary; and
Henceforth, all concerned City officials to strictly observe and abide with the provisions of RA No. 10121, NDRRMC-DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 and COA Circular          No. 2012-002.

We recommended the LCE, as the Chairperson of the PCCPC to:
Instruct the PCCPC to stop charging honoraria against the LCPC fund; and
Enjoin all members of the council to ensure proper utilization of the LCPC fund and strictly adhere to the rules and regulations governing the implementation of the PPAs of LCPC per Section 15 of RA No. 9344.

We recommended that the LCE require the City Accountant to:
Ensure remittance of all taxes withheld in compliance with tax laws and regulations to avoid under/over remittance and incurrence of penalties and surcharges; and
Ensure timely recording of taxes withheld and remittance made. Conduct reconciliation of the accounts on a monthly basis to determine causes of unreconciled amount and make the necessary adjustments to ensure accuracy of the ledger balances of the Due to BIR account at the end of each month.

We recommended that the LCE:
Strictly enforce compliance of audit suspensions and require persons responsible to submit the complete documentary requirements/justifications/explanations as required in the Notices of Suspensions; and
Enforce the immediate settlement of the audit disallowances and charges and initiate the necessary administrative and/or criminal action in case of unjustified failure/refusal to effect the settlement of disallowances and charges within the prescribed period in accordance with COA Circular No. 2009-006.

observations.managementAction:
The grant of TA to officials with issued/assigned service vehicles has been stopped.  




The service vehicles are used for official purpose or function of the office to where it was issued. 




Regular coordination is undertaken between GSO and HRMO to ensure that officials with assigned service vehicles are excluded from payment of transportation allowance.  




The required certification is adopted.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the LCE direct the CBO and CAO to review and analyze discrepancies noted in the utilization as reported in the SAAOB, appropriations instead of 
Statement of Financial Performance and the Report of SEF Utilization, and prepare necessary adjustments. Henceforth, ensure that reports are prepared and reconciled on a regular basis to avoid such discrepancies.
observations.managementAction: To correct the deficiencies noted, coordination among the offices of CAO, CBO and the School’s Division office is on-going to reconcile and correct the reported charging of accrued payables on capital outlay to current instead of to continuing appropriations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Accountant, together with the City Treasurer, prepare a thorough analysis and assessment of TF such as, review of the covering memoranda of agreement and/or contracts, if any, in order to assess the validity and propriety of these dormant account balances so that proper action and/or disposition may be done, and ensure that the balances reported in the financial statements are fairly presented.
observations.managementAction: CAO and CTO have conducted review and assessment of the accounts and thus, the dormant TF account balances have been closed and reverted to the general fund.
observations.reason: There were three out of the 12 dormant accounts that were already closed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE require the CAO, as well as the CTO, to perform immediate identification and thorough analysis/verification of the recorded trust liabilities and reconcile with the available cash balances in the TF books. Accordingly, the City Treasurer should also perform periodic reconciliation of the trust receipts/transfers coming into the possession of the City Treasurer with due regard on the proper utilization and charges against the TF for control and monitoring purposes.
observations.managementAction: CAO have identified the cause/s of the deficiency was due to payments erroneously charged to the General-Trust fund instead of the specific trust accounts. | CTO has yet to provide the CAO the details of fund transfers made within the trust funds for proper recording in the books to correct the deficiency.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the CAO and CTO identify and maintain a subsidiary ledger for each taxpayer who has tax credits and tax payments under protest to facilitate proper monitoring and recording in the books. Also, ensure that the application of said deposits as payment for RPT and License are properly taken up in the books.
observations.managementAction: CAO and CTO has started maintaining subsidiary ledger and records of tax payers with advance payment (tax credit) and tax payments under protest for proper monitoring and recording purposes.
observations.reason: The submitted lists of tax payers lack some information necessary for proper identification. CTO is working on to complete the data prior to submission to CAO.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the LCE direct the CAO and CTO to:

Revisit the City Ordinance covering the issuance of OVRs by the City traffic enforcers to determine the proper accounting treatment of said income; and

Reconcile variances noted and prepare necessary adjustments in the books, if warranted.
observations.managementAction:
Review of the Ordinance and contract is on-going to determine proper accounting of the revenue derived from the system/QPAX.

Recorded collections vis-à-vis abstract of collections submitted by CTO to CAO is in order/reconciled
observations.reason: CAO is waiting for the final result on the review of the contract to determine proper accounting and treatment of income on the issuance of OVRs and QPAX vis-a-vis recording thereof, whether on GF or TF.

observations.managementAction:
Management has not submitted comments/explanation on the subject audit observation.





Regular monitoring of on-going PPAs is now being undertaken by the City Engineering Office and the City Planning Office to ensure completion. 




Preparation and appropriation of funds on the City’s Annual Investment Plan is now judiciously being carried out by the City’s Finance Committee in compliance with the audit observation.

No action/ comment was received from management  






The City Engineer has directed all project engineers to monitor completion of all the infrastructure projects of the city (PPAs). 







The City has initially transferred the amount of P46,184,800.40 to the DF bank account from the GF bank account.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE direct the CAO, the CDRRMO and the CBO to analyze the cause/s of the variances noted between the RAAO and the accomplishment report, and make the necessary revisions in their respective registries/reports. Thereafter, perform periodic monitoring and reconciliation on the utilization of the LDRRMF.
observations.managementAction: Reconciliation of records between RAAO-LDRRMF maintained by the CAO and the Accomplishment Report of the CDRRMO is undertaken. Henceforth, regular/periodic monitoring and reconciliation on the utilization of the fund is being observed by CAO, CBO and the CDRRMO.

observations.recommendation:
Instruct the City Accountant and the City Budget Officer to review and analyze the DRRMF unexpended QRF and MOOE that should accrue to the Special TF; accordingly, the City Accountant to prepare the necessary JEV to correct the balances of the accounts both in TF and GF books; and

Direct the City Accountant, together with the City Treasurer, to perform immediate identification and thorough analysis of the recorded Trust Liability-DRRMF, and reconcile with the balance of the Cash in Bank-Calamity Fund in TF books.
observations.managementAction:
Appropriate adjustment was made in January 2021 in both the GF and TF books.





The CAO and CTO is in the process of completing the reconciliation of the account. Transfer of the unexpended QRF and MOOE for CY 2019 and 2020 has been made to CIB-Calamity Fund.
observations.reason: CAO and CTO is verifying the interest income transferred from TF to GF for proper adjustment.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management submit the revised LDRRMFIP as approved by the LCE and the City Council. Thereafter, changes in the approved LDRRMFIP should be supported with revised and/or supplemental LDRRMFIP through the passage/execution of an appropriation ordinance.
observations.managementAction: The CBO has said that the realignment was approved via zoom by the SP at a committee meeting level and the draft ordinance was eventually approved at a regular e-session by the SP
observations.reason: The copy of the ordinance and/or approval by the SP is not yet submitted to COA for review.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management direct the concerned City officials to:

Submit the revised/supplemental AIP, GPB and the GAD AR as approved by the City Council and the LCE, for our review and validation. Hereafter, changes in the approved GPB should be supported with the revised or supplemental GPB to be approved by the local sanggunian; 

Submit actual work completed on construction projects undertaken as at year-end for proper recording of obligations incurred. Henceforth, the CEO to prepare status of work accomplishments on all infrastructure projects bidded and implemented at year end; and

Administer the HGDG test on the GAD PPAs in the preparation of the GPB as basis for determining the estimated cost that can be attributed to the GAD budget. Henceforth, clearly provide qualitative and quantitative data that will support the gender related issues identified in the GPB.
observations.managementAction:
Appropriate corrections/revisions had been made by management. 





Projects undertaken were properly obligated based on actual accomplishment. Report on the status of work accomplishments on all infrastructure projects bidded and implemented at year end not yet prepared/submitted.




HGDG test on GAD PPAs were administered in CY 2021 GPB.
observations.reason: Report on the status of infrastructure projects not yet submitted by CEO for validation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE, in his capacity as the Chairman of the GFPS Executive Committee, direct the City officials concerned to fast track the completion of the GAD database containing the sex disaggregated data and other gender-related information that will support the gender issues identified and have these prioritized in the GAD Plan and Budget for the ensuring year/s.
observations.managementAction: The City Planning Officer commented that the GAD database was not yet fully established, they are using their Community-Based Monitoring System for GAD database and being used as basis in the preparation of the GPB.
observations.reason: GAD office is still in the process of updating and upgrading the GAD database. The delay was due to lack of manpower to help in the conduct of surveys and data   gathering.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Local Development Council (LDC) prepare and submit the revised AIP for the realigned funds as approved by the LCE and the City Council. Thereafter, changes in the approved AIP should be supported with supplemental AIP and the enactment of an appropriation ordinance authorizing the changes made therefrom.
observations.managementAction: The revised AIP was approved by the LCE and forwarded/ submitted to the City Council.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE direct the CTO, the GSO, and the CAO to submit the lacking supporting documents noted above.
observations.managementAction: The lacking supporting documents was submitted to CAO.

The taxes withheld were remitted in CY 2021. Henceforth, due diligence is being observed for timely remittance of taxes withheld to the BIR to avoid penalties and surcharges. 




Necessary adjusting Journal Entry were made by CAO in CY 2021.




The HRMO initiated the timely submission of supporting documents/payroll to CAO for proper recording in the books and ensure timely remittance of taxes withheld on compensation to the BIR to avoid payment of penalty and surcharge. 

Regular review and monitoring of the BIR (Due to BIR) accounts is being done.
observations.reason: Non-remittance of taxes in full on a monthly basis was still observed.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the LCE require the CAO and HRMO to:

Conduct detailed review, analysis and reconciliation on the unreconciled differences and unremitted prior year transactions, and make the necessary corrections/ adjustments in the GF and SEF books;

Remit monthly contributions, premium payments, loan amortizations and other amounts withheld from employees in full, including government share to the GSIS, Pag-IBIG and PhilHealth on time as prescribed by laws, rules and regulations to avoid possible imposition of interest, penalties and surcharges; and

Strengthen internal accounting and administrative controls on monitoring and review of withheld amounts including government share payable to GSIS,             Pag-IBIG and PhilHealth.
observations.managementAction:
CAO and HRMO are in the process of reconciling the accounts with their records for proper corrective measures/adjustment necessary. 




For CY 2021, timely remittance of monthly contributions is being made and monitored. For the discrepancy, CAO and HRMO is in the process of reconciling the accounts with their records for proper corrective measures/adjustment necessary. 




Appropriate accounting and administrative controls was put in place to ensure correct computation, recording and on-time remittance of amounts withheld plus government share to GSIS, Pag-IBIG and PhilHealth.
observations.reason:
Reconciliation of the accounts is still ongoing.





Same observation was noted in CY 2021.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Officials:

Strictly comply with Items 4.1.6, 5.1 and 5.8 of COA Circular No. 97-002 on the grant, utilization and liquidation of cash advances; and

Coordinate with officials and employees of the DepEd for the reconstruction of the documents for the liquidation of unaccounted cash advances.
observations.managementAction:
Compliance with Items 4.1.6, 5.1 and 5.8 of COA Circular No. 97-002 on the grant, utilization and liquidation of cash advances is enforced.

Management is coordinating with DepEd officials and employees with outstanding cash advances.
observations.reason:
Same observation was noted in CY 2021.





Demand letters were sent to DepEd officials and employees with outstanding cash advances.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management revisit the International Public Sector Accounting Standards (IPSAS) for the recording of demolished structures based on replacements costs, if documents for the old Legislative Building could no longer be located.
observations.managementAction: Appropriate adjustment has already been made in accordance with IPSAS.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor as Local Chief Executive:

Exert effort to ensure that the 20% development fund of the City is efficiently utilized and that the implementation of all identified PPAs be given priority to warrant the full attainment of goals for the betterment of the social and economic conditions of the constituents in accordance with DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1; and

Monitor regularly the progress of each project implementation to achieve the desired level of efficiency.
observations.managementAction:
PPAs charged against the 20% DF for CY 2019 has been fully utilized/implemented in CY 2019 and CY 2020.  







PPAs relating to the 20% DF are being monitored by assigned project Engineer to ensure timely completion.

Management’s GAD Focal Point system submitted the GPB together with the accomplishment report for CY 2019 and 2020.  

GFPS ensures that GAD related programs/issues are incorporated in the GPB and projects are properly coordinated with implementing units to ensure timely completion of the GSD PPAs. 

The City Planning Officer commented that the GAD database was not yet fully established, they are using their Community-Based Monitoring System for GAD database and being used as basis in the preparation of the GPB.
observations.reason: GAD office is still in the process of updating and upgrading the GAD database. The delay was due to lack of manpower to help in the conduct of surveys and data   gathering.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the Department Heads to conduct adequate planning of the work activities of their staff and see to it that individual work assignments stated in their request for authority to render overtime services are thoroughly reviewed before recommending the same for approval of the City Mayor.
observations.managementAction: Management ensures that authority to render overtime is in compliance with CSC and DBM Joint Circular. The grant of authority to render overtime is given only under exceptional/meritorious cases.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the OIC-City Treasurer’s Office (CTO) to fast track the remittance of trust collections of the City for and in behalf of the other government agencies and that the City Accountant exert effort in retrieving the documents that may support the payable account to the MMDA so that the same may be remitted.
observations.managementAction: CAO has submitted the liquidation report and related supporting documents to MMDA.  Management has yet to receive copy of the debit advice from MMDA.
observations.reason: The balance has been reduced to P27,477.48 representing payable to MMDA. CAO will prepare the corresponding JEV once the debit advice is received.
